data.short_description: Romance genre pack for the Novel Studio app: spark, wound, and rift thread types, plus relationship-arc review lenses. A novel adopts the pack in its Blueprint; its threads may then use these types, and reviews run these lenses.

lore.title: Thread Types
lore.content:
Additional thread_type values for novels that adopt this pack. Exact spellings; each entry says what the type is and what its payoff owes the reader.

spark — a moment of genuine connection between the leads: the glance, the argument that turns, the kindness no one else saw. Its payoff is an echo: sparks planted early should be recalled or fulfilled at the commitment, so the ending rhymes with the beginning.

wound — the belief or old hurt that keeps a lead from love ("everyone leaves", "I am the responsible one, not the loved one"). One per lead, usually. Its payoff is confrontation: the wound must be faced on the page before the happy ending — an ending that skips it feels unearned because it is. A lead's wound thread usually restates the character's own lie and ghost properties — point it at the same wording; don't fork a second version.

rift — the fault line that will crack the couple at the dark moment: the withheld truth, the competing loyalty, the deal one of them made. Planted early, its payoff comes in two parts — the eruption (the break-up scene) and the repair (what the grand gesture must actually answer).

lore.title: Review Lenses
lore.content:
Checks the Review workflow runs for novels that adopt this pack. Report per lens, citing scenes and threads by slug; findings are surfaced, never auto-fixed.

ANCHORS: the leads are named in the novel's Blueprint when this pack is adopted — record them there at adoption; when the Blueprint doesn't name them, ask now and record them before running these lenses. The dark moment is the rift thread's eruption scene and the grand gesture its repair, both named in the rift's Thread lore as they land; when unnamed, ask rather than guess.

Dual-POV Balance — count scenes by pov across the leads. Romance runs on both interiorities: one lead carrying nearly every scene while the other stays opaque is a finding (unless the Blueprint declares a single-POV plan — then skip this lens).

Relationship Ladder — walk the leads' shared scenes in sequence order: does emotional intimacy move step by step (meeting, friction, trust, vulnerability, love), or does it jump a rung offstage? A leap with no scene under it is a finding.

Wound Work — each lead's wound thread must be pressed by at least one scene before the dark moment and confronted before the ending. A wound that never gets touched is a finding: it means the character had no reason to change.

Earned Ending — read the grand-gesture and commitment scenes against the rift and wound threads: does the gesture answer the actual rift (the thing that broke them) and honor the wounds, or is it a generic apology with flowers? A gesture that resolves nothing named is a finding.
